title: nama hispida var. spathulata
intro: Nama hispida var. spathulata, also known as desert heliotrope, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub with small, fragrant flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Nama hispida var. spathulata can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
